------------------------------------------------------------------------

### render_virtual_collection

``` python

def render_virtual_collection(
    items:list, # Full item list
    config:VirtualCollectionConfig, # Collection config
    state:VirtualCollectionState, # Collection state
    ids:VirtualCollectionHtmlIds, # HTML IDs
    urls:VirtualCollectionUrls, # URL bundle
    render_cell:Optional=None, # Table layout cell render callback
    render_item:Optional=None, # Grid layout item render callback
    render_empty:Optional=None, # Empty state callback: () -> FT component
)->functools.partial(<function ft_hx at 0x7f13d16923e0>, 'div'): # Complete collection element

```

*Render a complete virtual collection with wrapper, table, scrollbar,
and footer.*

## Tests

``` python
from dataclasses import dataclass
from fasthtml.common import to_xml, Span
from cjm_fasthtml_virtual_collection.core.models import (
    VirtualCollectionConfig, VirtualCollectionState, ColumnDef, VirtualCollectionUrls,
)
from cjm_fasthtml_virtual_collection.core.html_ids import VirtualCollectionHtmlIds

@dataclass
class Item:
    name: str
    size: str

test_items = [Item(f"file_{i}.txt", f"{i}KB") for i in range(20)]

def test_render_cell(item, ctx):
    if ctx.column.key == "name": return Span(item.name)
    if ctx.column.key == "size": return Span(item.size)

columns = (
    ColumnDef(key="name", header="Name"),
    ColumnDef(key="size", header="Size"),
)
config = VirtualCollectionConfig(prefix="tc", columns=columns)
state = VirtualCollectionState(total_items=20, visible_rows=5)
ids = VirtualCollectionHtmlIds(prefix=config.prefix)
urls = VirtualCollectionUrls()
```

``` python
# Test full collection render
collection = render_virtual_collection(
    items=test_items, config=config, state=state,
    ids=ids, urls=urls, render_cell=test_render_cell,
)
html = to_xml(collection)

# Outer container with flex-child classes
assert 'id="tc-collection"' in html
assert 'grow-1' in html or 'grow' in html
assert 'min-h-0' in html

# Wrapper (viewport-fit target)
assert 'id="tc-wrapper"' in html
assert 'overflow-y-hidden' in html

# Table
assert 'id="tc-table"' in html

# Header (inside table-header-group)
assert 'id="tc-header"' in html
assert 'table-header-group' in html
assert 'Name' in html
assert 'Size' in html

# Rows (table-row-group)
assert 'id="tc-rows"' in html
assert 'table-row-group' in html

# Visible rows (0-4, visible_rows=5)
assert 'id="tc-row-0"' in html
assert 'id="tc-row-4"' in html
assert 'id="tc-row-5"' not in html

# Slots with display:contents
assert 'id="tc-slot-0"' in html
assert 'contents' in html

# Cell IDs
assert 'id="tc-row-0-col-name"' in html
assert 'id="tc-row-0-col-size"' in html

# Scrollbar (20 items > 5 visible)
assert 'id="tc-scrollbar-track"' in html
assert 'id="tc-scrollbar-thumb"' in html

# Footer
assert 'id="tc-footer"' in html
assert 'Showing 1-5 of 20 items' in html

# Cell content
assert 'file_0.txt' in html
assert 'file_4.txt' in html
assert 'file_5.txt' not in html

print("Full collection render test passed")
```

``` python
# Test with window_start offset
state2 = VirtualCollectionState(total_items=20, visible_rows=5, window_start=10)
collection2 = render_virtual_collection(
    items=test_items, config=config, state=state2,
    ids=ids, urls=urls, render_cell=test_render_cell,
)
html2 = to_xml(collection2)

assert 'id="tc-row-10"' in html2
assert 'id="tc-row-14"' in html2
assert 'id="tc-row-9"' not in html2
assert 'id="tc-row-15"' not in html2
assert 'Showing 11-15 of 20 items' in html2
assert 'file_10.txt' in html2

print("Offset window render test passed")
```

    Offset window render test passed

``` python
# Test empty state with render_empty callback
import re
from fasthtml.common import P

def my_empty_state():
    return P("No files found", id="empty-msg")

empty_state = VirtualCollectionState(total_items=0, visible_rows=5)
collection_empty = render_virtual_collection(
    items=[], config=config, state=empty_state,
    ids=ids, urls=urls, render_cell=test_render_cell,
    render_empty=my_empty_state,
)
html_empty = to_xml(collection_empty)

# Empty state component is rendered
assert 'No files found' in html_empty
assert 'id="empty-msg"' in html_empty

# Header is still present
assert 'id="tc-header"' in html_empty
assert 'Name' in html_empty

# No data rows
assert 'id="tc-row-0"' not in html_empty

# Scrollbar present but hidden (0 items <= 5 visible)
assert 'id="tc-scrollbar-track"' in html_empty
assert 'hidden' in html_empty

# Footer shows "No items"
assert 'No items' in html_empty

# ── Structural regression guards (V8 / G5+G7 placement) ────────────────
# The empty content must NOT be a child of the table-display Div (which
# would constrain it via CSS table layout — cell-anonymization, intrinsic-
# height collapse, etc.). Instead, empty content lives as a sibling of the
# table inside the wrapper, with the wrapper as a flex column so the
# empty-region's grow() fills the vertical space below the header-only
# table. This is the G5 + G7 placement convention surfaced 2026-05-14.

# Wrapper Div includes flex + flex-col so the empty region's grow()
# resolves against the wrapper's available height.
wrapper_open_match = re.search(r'<div[^>]*id="tc-wrapper"[^>]*>', html_empty)
assert wrapper_open_match, f"tc-wrapper open tag not found in: {html_empty[:300]!r}"
wrapper_open_tag = wrapper_open_match.group(0)
assert 'flex-col' in wrapper_open_tag, (
    f"Wrapper must include flex-col class (so the empty region's grow() can "
    f"fill space below the header-only table). "
    f"Got wrapper open tag: {wrapper_open_tag!r}"
)

# Empty content must come AFTER the table div's closing — i.e., it's a sibling,
# not a child of tc-table. We approximate via positional check: the empty
# content marker appears in the HTML at a position after both the table's
# open tag AND the header-group's nested close. A more rigorous DOM-tree
# walk would require a parser; the positional check catches the regression
# where empty content drifts back inside the table-display Div.
table_open_pos = html_empty.find('id="tc-table"')
header_open_pos = html_empty.find('id="tc-header"')
empty_msg_pos = html_empty.find('id="empty-msg"')
assert table_open_pos >= 0 and header_open_pos >= 0 and empty_msg_pos >= 0, (
    "Required structural markers missing"
)
assert header_open_pos < empty_msg_pos, (
    "Header must appear before empty content in the HTML stream (header inside "
    f"table-display; empty content sibling-below). Got positions: "
    f"header={header_open_pos}, empty={empty_msg_pos}"
)

# ── Wrapper-class invariant: always flex-col ───────────────────────────
# Both empty and populated cases share the same wrapper class. This is
# load-bearing for the mutation path — build_items_changed_response uses
# hx-swap-oob='innerHTML' to preserve the wrapper element identity (and
# its inline height styles from viewport-fit). Since innerHTML swap
# doesn't touch the wrapper's class, the class must work for both
# states; flex-col is therefore always-on.
populated_state = VirtualCollectionState(total_items=20, visible_rows=5)
collection_populated = render_virtual_collection(
    items=test_items, config=config, state=populated_state,
    ids=ids, urls=urls, render_cell=test_render_cell,
    render_empty=my_empty_state,  # ignored when total_items > 0
)
html_populated = to_xml(collection_populated)
populated_wrapper_match = re.search(r'<div[^>]*id="tc-wrapper"[^>]*>', html_populated)
assert populated_wrapper_match, "tc-wrapper open tag not found in populated render"
populated_wrapper_tag = populated_wrapper_match.group(0)
assert 'flex-col' in populated_wrapper_tag, (
    f"Wrapper must include flex-col class in populated case too — both empty "
    f"and populated states share the wrapper class so innerHTML-OOB swap on "
    f"the wrapper's children doesn't need to toggle the class. "
    f"Got wrapper open tag: {populated_wrapper_tag!r}"
)

# CSS table inside the flex-column wrapper still renders correctly:
# the table-display Div is a single flex item with intrinsic height +
# w.full width. Body-group is present, slots are rendered.
assert 'id="tc-rows"' in html_populated
assert 'table-row-group' in html_populated
assert 'id="tc-row-0"' in html_populated

print("Empty state render test passed")
```

    Empty state render test passed
